name: Yext
heading: Yext Product Overview
highlight_text: Who it is best for: Enterprise and mid-market customers
product_overview_description:
Yext Product Overview

Yext is one of the oldest software in the industry, with very strong capabilities for listing management. The platform is heavily focused on big agencies and resellers, with many expanded offerings adjacent to local marketing. Customer support is something that customers usually complain about, given the massive scale of operations.

‍

‍

Core Features

  • Access to 200+ third-party publishers for business data distribution
  • Dual-sync direct API integrations for real-time updates
  • Consolidated Listings Analytics dashboard
  • Real-time update architecture across publishers
  • Duplicate listing deactivation (100+ listings)
  • Incorporation of suggested edits from Google, Facebook, and users
  • Competitive performance insights
  • Image distribution across listings
  • Comprehensive tracking of listing statuses and error alerts
  • Automated Listings Verifier for accuracy checks
  • Reputation management tool that helps you monitor and respond to reviews on Google and Facebook
  • Custom field support for rich content
  • Granular custom controls for listing management
  • Local landing pages to create a hyper-local presence for all your locations.

‍

‍

What’s great

✅Leverage an extensive network to ensure your business information is consistently visible across the web.

✅Keep your business information current and responsive to market changes to build customer trust and satisfaction.

✅Present a unified, accurate business image by eliminating confusing duplicate listings and inconsistencies.

✅Save time and resources with automated processes that maintain your online presence with minimal manual intervention.

✅Built for scale with custom support for agencies and resellers that partner with Yext

✅Platform is robust, with minimum technical glitches and issues making it easy to handle thousands of locations smoothly.

✅Whitelabeling is available for agencies and resellers

‍

What’s not great

❌Customer support seems to be a major pain point, especially for small-mid sized businesses that struggle to get flexible plans and get tangled in support tickets

❌Platform is very expensive, especially if you’re trying to add their reputation management feature, you’ll end up paying thousands of dollars a year and get stuck in annualized contracts

❌Lacks continuous innovation. The platform very outdated for its core features and the massive scale of operations mean there’s little to no innovation to add value for customers.

‍

Pricing

Yext doesn’t offer any free trial for their regular plans, but for their enterprise plan there might some sort of a trial plan, however it’s not clear on their website. The paid plans are billed annually, starting at around $16 per month per location for a very limited set of directories. For more than 10 locations, you’ll have to reach out to their support team. The reseller pricing for Yext differs from regular price structure.
